mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and supports higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month growth versus 20-day forward returns is negative or below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output and economic strength, which translates into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO year-over-year growth has a negative correlation with subsequent 60-day SPX returns over any 2-year period, or mean return in months with INDPRO > median is not statistically greater than in months below median.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) crossing above 5% signals labor market deterioration, reducing consumer confidence and spending, and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 5%, the 5-day forward SPX return is positive more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) crosses above 5.25%, it indicates tightening financial conditions that raise discount rates and borrowing costs, depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: After FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.25%, the average 20-day forward SPX return is non-negative, or the proportion of positive returns is above 50% with a statistical significance level of 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) indicate that monetary policy is tightening reactively to inflation, raising stagflation fears and depressing equity prices, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: Both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL three-month change > 0
failure_condition: In periods when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL are rising, the 60-day forward SPX return is positive more than half the time, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1400 thousand units and retail sales (RSAFS) below 360 million dollars signals a synchronized demand contraction, which historically precedes recessions and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: When both HOUST and RSAFS are below their respective thresholds, the 60-day forward SPX return is positive in more than 40% of instances, or the mean return is not statistically negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle defined by FEDFUNDS above 5%, rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) confirm that the economy is resilient enough to withstand higher rates, which supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, month-over-month growth in PAYEMS has a negative correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns, or the average return following above-median payroll growth is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In an economic contraction regime where GDP (GDPC1) has contracted for two consecutive quarters, a positive GDP growth surprise (quarterly increase) signals the trough has passed, leading to strong positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: economic contraction (GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth negative for two quarters)
failure_condition: During periods of two consecutive quarterly GDP contractions, the 60-day forward SPX return following a positive GDP quarter is negative in more than 40% of cases, or the average return is not significantly above zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).
